prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for aniline were computed using the fukui-reactivity skill at the DFT level, tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, exact four-centre integrals, no density fitting), in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N−1 (cation) state used charge +1, multiplicity 2 (doublet); the N+1 (anion) state used charge −1, multiplicity 2 (doublet). Partial charges were obtained via Mulliken population analysis. Geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied aniline.xyz (not re-optimised). All three SCF calculations converged; integrity status is 'ok', trustworthy: true. Fukui charge conservation: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000. No warnings were reported.

Key results (atom numbering 1-based):
  Most nucleophilic site: Atom 1 (N), f⁻ = 0.20452359 [headline value]
  Most electrophilic site: Atom 3 (C, ortho), f⁺ = 2.2975867
  Dual descriptor Δf most electrophilic: Atom 3 (C, ortho), Δf = 2.2310153

The analysis confirms aniline's canonical reactivity: the amino nitrogen is the dominant nucleophilic/Lewis-basic centre, while the ortho and para ring carbons are the preferred sites for electrophilic aromatic substitution — consistent with the ortho/para-directing, ring-activating nature of the −NH₂ group.

CLI invocation: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1
Output JSON: aniline_fukui_dft.json
Bar-chart plot: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260704-045534_aniline_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png
